summary: Four docs-verified changes driven by new official material: documents the on-by-default auto-memory system, AGENTS.md interop, and compaction instructions in CLAUDE.md; corrects the subagent CLAUDE.md-loading claim (only Explore/Plan skip it). Practitioner sources quiet or corroborating.

changes.item: Subagent CLAUDE.md loading: most subagents (custom and built-in) inherit your full CLAUDE.md/memory hierarchy; only the built-in Explore and Plan agents skip it (and git status) to stay fast — restate any rule they must follow in the delegation prompt.
changes.rationale: Accuracy correction. The sub-agents docs now state this precisely; the prior edition's 'each subagent starts blind / Explore and Plan also skip CLAUDE.md' framing was overbroad and implied all subagents skip it.

changes.item: Auto memory (on by default, v2.1.59+) is a second memory system Claude maintains itself in ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/; the first 200 lines (or 25KB) of MEMORY.md load each session. It complements the CLAUDE.md you curate; toggle via /memory.
changes.rationale: Newly documented, on-by-default mechanism distinct from CLAUDE.md — every user now has it, so the template should name it.

changes.item: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md. If your repo already uses AGENTS.md, import it (@AGENTS.md) or symlink it (ln -s AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md) rather than maintaining two files.
changes.rationale: New interop guidance in the memory docs; common real situation as AGENTS.md adoption grows across agent tools.

changes.item: You can bake compaction behaviour into CLAUDE.md itself (e.g. 'When compacting, always preserve the list of modified files and any test commands') so critical context survives every summarisation.
changes.rationale: New best-practices guidance; extends the existing /compact line beyond per-invocation '/compact Focus on [X]'.

excluded_detail.item: ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1 enables a 1-hour prompt-cache TTL for long sessions (knightli).
excluded_detail.reason: Accuracy gate: env var not found anywhere in the official docs.

excluded_detail.item: Automatic compaction triggers at ~95% of the context window (Marmelab).
excluded_detail.reason: Accuracy gate: docs describe compaction firing 'when context approaches the limit' with no numeric threshold; the 95% figure is unverifiable.

excluded_detail.item: Keep stable rules at the top of CLAUDE.md for prompt-cache hits (knightli).
excluded_detail.reason: Same plausible-but-undocumented cache-ordering claim cut in a prior edition; still not in the docs. Stays excluded.

notes: Docs-led cycle. The official docs shipped substantial new material since 2026-06-08; the accuracy gate verified 9 of 11 shortlisted technical claims verbatim and failed 2 (above). Verified-but-held (too granular or in-the-weeds for a universal template): CLAUDE.md is delivered as a user message after the system prompt + --append-system-prompt (provisional); custom commands merged into skills; path-scoped skills (paths frontmatter); /goal condition fitness criteria; InstructionsLoaded hook; hook context phrased as factual statements; $schema for settings.json. Practitioner sources: Simon Willison, Peter Steinberger (joined OpenAI; blog dormant, substack 404), forrestchang/karpathy CLAUDE.md (unchanged since 2026-01-27), Matt Pocock — no new in-window guidance. Held Tier-2 single-source items: Addy Osmani (intent ledger / tier review by blast radius / agents rewrite test assertions), obra/superpowers v6.0.0 (name the model when delegating, pass work as files, read-only uncoachable reviewers, plan Global Constraints + Interfaces, task right-sizing, extended-thinking keyword gotcha, vendor-neutral phrasing), Nick Nisi (attention is the multi-agent bottleneck). Tier-3 held: Stanford CS336 CLAUDE.md specimen (should/should-not + Good/Bad examples), 'Daily Driver' blog. Bluesky unreachable (403).

summary: Three verified additions from Anthropic's docs — path-scoped .claude/rules/, concrete 'when to add' triggers, and the /goal + Stop-hook (8-block) verification gates. A quiet cycle for practitioner sources.

changes.item: As CLAUDE.md grows, split rules into .claude/rules/*.md; a rule with a paths: glob loads only when Claude reads a matching file — path-scoped, so you don't pay context for it until it's relevant.
changes.rationale: New in the official memory docs; extends the context-budget guidance.

changes.item: Concrete 'when to add to CLAUDE.md' triggers: Claude makes the same mistake twice, a review catches what it should've known, or a new teammate would need the same context.
changes.rationale: New heuristic in the official memory docs; sharpens the existing 'treat like code / update' guidance.

changes.item: Verification gates: a Stop hook blocks the turn until your check passes (Claude Code ends it after 8 consecutive blocks); or a /goal condition with a separate evaluator that keeps Claude working until it holds.
changes.rationale: The /goal evaluator and the '8 consecutive blocks' override are now confirmed in the official best-practices docs — the 8-block claim FAILED verification last edition and PASSES now.

notes: Incremental run, 4 days after Edition 2026-06-04. Practitioner sources quiet (Simon Willison, Marmelab, Matt Pocock, obra/superpowers, Nick Nisi: 0 in-window). Anthropic docs shipped multiple changes; Addy Osmani published 2 relevant pieces. Verified-but-held (too advanced for a universal template): managed/org CLAUDE.md, claudeMdExcludes, InstructionsLoaded hook, subagent isolation:worktree / memory: frontmatter, --permission-mode auto, --append-system-prompt. Also held: Addy 'Loop Engineering' + HN/knightli skill-design (Tier 3).

summary: First published edition. Added verified guidance on checkpoint recovery, compaction survival, subagent scoping, turn-gating hooks, fresh-context review, and permission enforcement. One plausible cache claim was excluded for failing verification.

changes.item: Recover instead of fighting a bad path: /rewind (or double-Esc on an empty prompt) rolls back code and/or conversation; /btw asks throwaway questions that never enter history.
changes.rationale: Both commands and the Esc-Esc shortcut are confirmed verbatim in the official interactive-mode and checkpointing docs.

changes.item: Project-root CLAUDE.md re-injects after /compact; nested CLAUDE.md files and chat-only instructions do not — keep must-survive rules in the root file.
changes.rationale: Stated explicitly in the official memory documentation.

changes.item: Subagents start blind to your session — scope them narrowly; the built-in Explore and Plan agents skip CLAUDE.md, so restate any rule they must follow in the delegation prompt.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official sub-agents documentation.

changes.item: A Stop hook can block a turn from ending until your check script succeeds — deterministic where CLAUDE.md is only advisory.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official hooks documentation.

changes.item: Before declaring done, have a fresh subagent review the diff against the requirements; tell it to flag only gaps affecting correctness or stated requirements, or it will invent work.
changes.rationale: Reflects the review-subagent guidance in the official best-practices documentation.

changes.item: Permissions are enforced by Claude Code, not the model: a deny rule or PreToolUse hook blocks an action regardless of what CLAUDE.md or the prompt says.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official permissions documentation.

changes.item: Block-level HTML comments are stripped before CLAUDE.md loads — free for maintainer notes, unlike prose and '#' lines which consume tokens.
changes.rationale: Confirmed in the official memory documentation.

changes.item: Plan mode is worth it for multi-file or uncertain-scope work, but skip it when you could describe the diff in one sentence.
changes.rationale: Reflects the planning guidance in the official best-practices documentation.

changes.item: Line-budget guidance now carries a concrete test: for each line ask 'would removing this cause a mistake?' If not, cut it. Target aligned to under 200 lines.
changes.rationale: Matches the official memory docs (under 200 lines; bloated files reduce adherence), corroborated by community sources.

excluded_detail.item: Put stable rules at the top of CLAUDE.md and volatile notes at the bottom to maximise prompt-cache hits.
excluded_detail.reason: Not stated in the official docs — prompt-cache hit-rate ordering is community inference, not documented Claude Code behaviour. Failed the accuracy gate.
